data.title: Michael Saylor: No Tariffs on Bitcoin
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) founder Michael Saylor posted on the X platform that there is no tariff on Bitcoin.
Earlier news, Strategy acquired 22,048 BTC again for approximately US$1.92 billion between March 24 and March 30, at a price of approximately US$86,969 per Bitcoin, and has achieved a return of 11.0% so far in 2025. As of March 31, 2025, Strategy holds 528,185 BTC, acquired at a price of approximately US$35.63 billion, at a price of approximately US$67,458 per Bitcoin.

data.description: OKX market data shows that BTC fell below 82,000 USDT and is now at 81,990.4 USDT, a 24-hour drop of 3.39%. (This newsletter is generated with the assistance of AI)

data.title: In the past hour, the entire network has liquidated 57.8323 million US dollars, mainly long orders

data.title: BNB Drops Below 590 USDT with a 1.81% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 03, 2025, 12:44 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 590 USDT and is now trading at 589.650024 USDT, with a narrowed 1.81% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Analyst: Trump's reciprocal tariffs may create buying opportunities in the crypto market, BTC may rise to $90,000
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News The Kobeissi Letter analyzed that shortly after Trump announced the most stringent tariff measures in U.S. history, S&P 500 futures fell more than 2%, and the market value evaporated more than $2 trillion. Driven by rumors of delayed tariffs, Bitcoin rose to about $88,000 before the news was announced. After the tariff news was announced, BTC quickly gave up its gains and fell to $82,000.
BRN Chief Analyst Valentin Fournier believes that although Trump’s massive tariffs have shaken almost all financial markets, the much-anticipated clarification of U.S. trade policy has created buying opportunities because uncertainty is fading. Despite recent volatility, uncertainty is decreasing and institutional buying pressure is picking up. With the synergy of key catalysts, we expect Bitcoin to rebuild momentum and try $90,000 again in the near future. (The Block)

data.title: U.S. Trade Negotiations Continue Amid Tariff Discussions
data.description: According to PANews, U.S. Commerce Secretary Lutnick stated on April 3 that negotiations are ongoing with all major countries worldwide. He emphasized that there will likely be no exemptions from tariffs, and retaliatory measures by other nations against U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs are expected to be ineffective.

data.title: U.S. Weekly Jobless Claims Fall Below Expectations
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the number of initial jobless claims in the United States for the week ending March 29 was reported at 219,000. This figure is lower than the anticipated 225,000 claims, indicating a slight improvement in the labor market.

data.title: U.S. Commerce Secretary Predicts Lower Interest Rates
data.description: According to Odaily, U.S. Commerce Secretary Wilbur Ross has stated that interest rates in the United States are expected to be significantly lower. He also mentioned that there would be no exemptions regarding tariffs, and retaliatory measures by other countries against tariffs imposed by U.S. President Donald Trump would not be effective.

data.title: Bitget Launchpool will list StakeStone (STO)
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Bitget is about to launch the Launchpool project StakeStone (STO), with a total reward pool of 3,150,000 STOs. The lock-up opening time is from 21:00 on April 3 to 21:00 on April 4 (UTC+8). BGB lock-up pool: 
Ordinary user staking limit: 5000 BGB 
VIP user staking limit: 30,000 BGB

data.title: U.S. Commerce Secretary: U.S. interest rates will be much lower
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: U.S. Commerce Secretary Lutnick said that U.S. interest rates will be much lower. He also previously said that he believed there would be no exemptions (for tariffs) and that retaliation by other countries against Trump's tariffs would not be effective. (Jinshi)
